mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) signal expanding employment income and aggregate demand, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive month-over-month changes in PAYEMS do not associate with positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in greater than 55% of months over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 4.5%, restrictive monetary policy depresses equity valuations through higher discount rates and slower credit growth, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in greater than 50% of instances when FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.5% over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in UNRATE and CPIAUCSL indicate stagflationary pressure where slowing labor markets coincide with rising inflation, compressing profit margins and policy flexibility, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: When both UNRATE and CPIAUCSL rise month-over-month, S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive more than 50% of the time over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: During a monetary tightening cycle, month-over-month declines in RSAFS signal that higher interest rates are constraining consumer spending, leading to downward revenue revisions and negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: In identified tightening cycles, negative RSAFS months fail to associate with negative 20-day S&P 500 returns in greater than 55% of cases over a rolling 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in INDPRO signal improving capacity utilization and operating leverage, which drives positive earnings surprises and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive month-over-month changes in INDPRO do not correlate with positive 5-day S&P 500 returns with a Rank IC above 0.03 over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained levels of HOUST below 1800 thousand units signal that the housing market is entering a downturn, dragging construction, mortgage credit, and consumer wealth, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: When HOUST is below 1800, S&P 500 60-day returns are positive in greater than 50% of triggered instances over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in CPIAUCSL exceeding 0.3% signal unexpectedly high inflation that raises real discount rates and reduces equity cash flows, producing negative 20-day S&P 500 returns during a monetary tightening cycle.
failure_condition: Large CPIAUCSL monthly increases do not precede negative 20-day S&P 500 returns in greater than 55% of cases during tightening cycles over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month declines in UNRATE exceeding 0.1 percentage point signal an overheating labor market that amplifies wage-push inflation fears, causing the market to price in more aggressive Federal Reserve tightening and generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: UNRATE declines do not precede negative 5-day S&P 500 returns in greater than 55% of triggered instances over a 2-year rolling window.
